name: ResNet (Deep Residual Network)
training_approach: Supervised learning on ImageNet with residual (skip) connections enabling training of very deep networks
key_innovations: Introduced residual (skip) connections that allow training of networks with 100+ layers
Solved the degradation problem — deeper networks had been performing worse than shallow ones
Won ILSVRC 2015 with 3.57% top-5 error (first to surpass human-level performance on ImageNet)
Skip connections became a standard component in virtually all deep architectures including Transformers
lineage_path: LeNet -> AlexNet -> VGG -> ResNet -> ResNeXt -> DenseNet
cultural_impact: ResNet's skip connections are one of the most important architectural innovations in deep learning history. By enabling the training of very deep networks, ResNet broke through a fundamental barrier and became the standard backbone for computer vision. The residual connection concept was later adopted in Transformers (as residual connections around attention and feed-forward layers), making ResNet an intellectual ancestor of modern LLMs.
